county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created 1820 from Clark, Jackson, Jefferson, Jennings, Washington.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Scottsburg. Created 1820 from Clark, Jackson, Jefferson, Jennings, and Washington counties — many parent counties to check. Scott County Historical Society. Muscatatuck River and Stucker Fork settlements. BLM-GLO federal land patents. Scottsburg and Austin area records. One of Indiana's smaller counties. Strong Baptist and Methodist church records. Pigeon Roost State Historic Site (War of 1812 massacre). Check all five parent counties for pre-1820 records. Well-preserved but limited early records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1820. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Indiana deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — earliest deeds may reference BLM-GLO original patents.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage applications, licenses, and returns from 1820. Indiana County Clerk issues marriage licenses. Applications include ages, birthplaces, and parents' names (improving detail over time).
source_profiles.survival_notes: Well-preserved at County Clerk office

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, administrations, guardianships, and estate inventories from 1820. Indiana probate is handled by the Circuit Court (County Clerk). Inventories often list personal property in detail and name heirs.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Probate records generally well-preserved at county level

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Civil and criminal case files, order books, and judgments from 1820. Indiana Circuit Court is the general trial court. Early records include debt cases, property disputes, bastardy cases, and naturalization petitions (pre-1906) that identify family relationships. Order books often record road petitions naming landowners.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Court records generally well-preserved at county level

source_profiles.repository: Scott County Auditor, Scottsburg / Indiana State Archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Tax duplicates list property owners, land descriptions, and property values from 1820. Indiana county auditor records. Early tax lists serve as census substitutes. Indiana State Archives holds some microfilmed county tax records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Some tax duplicates at Indiana State Archives; others remain at county level

source_profiles.source_name: Clark's Grant Land Records — Scott County
source_profiles.repository: Indiana State Archives, Indianapolis / Indiana State Library
source_profiles.coverage_notes: George Rogers Clark's Illinois Regiment land grant (150,000 acres) in southeastern Indiana. Soldiers who served under Clark in the Revolutionary War received land warrants. This area was NOT part of the federal land system — use Virginia/Illinois records, not BLM-GLO. Early Clarksville (1784) was Indiana's first American settlement.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Records at Indiana State Archives and Indiana State Library; some published in Indiana land record compilations

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Shelbyville. Created 1821 from unorganized territory (Delaware). Shelby County Historical Society in Shelbyville. Blue River and Brandywine Creek valley settlements. BLM-GLO federal land patents. Strong Quaker heritage — Shelby County had early Friends meetings. Major Grizzly Bear (Joseph Barton) historical figure. Charles Major (author, 'When Knighthood Was in Flower') from Shelbyville. Morristown and Waldron area records. County Clerk holds marriage records from 1822. Well-preserved county records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1821. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Indiana deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — earliest deeds may reference BLM-GLO original patents.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage applications, licenses, and returns from 1821. Indiana County Clerk issues marriage licenses. Applications include ages, birthplaces, and parents' names (improving detail over time).
source_profiles.survival_notes: Well-preserved at County Clerk office

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, administrations, guardianships, and estate inventories from 1821. Indiana probate is handled by the Circuit Court (County Clerk). Inventories often list personal property in detail and name heirs.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Probate records generally well-preserved at county level

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Civil and criminal case files, order books, and judgments from 1821. Indiana Circuit Court is the general trial court. Early records include debt cases, property disputes, bastardy cases, and naturalization petitions (pre-1906) that identify family relationships. Order books often record road petitions naming landowners.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Court records generally well-preserved at county level

source_profiles.repository: Shelby County Auditor, Shelbyville / Indiana State Archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Tax duplicates list property owners, land descriptions, and property values from 1821. Indiana county auditor records. Early tax lists serve as census substitutes. Indiana State Archives holds some microfilmed county tax records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Some tax duplicates at Indiana State Archives; others remain at county level

source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Original federal land patents for Shelby County. Indiana is a federal land state — most land was sold through federal land offices. Patents record first purchaser from the government. Searchable online at glorecords.blm.gov. Cross-reference with county deed records for subsequent transfers.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Complete federal records — well-preserved and digitized at BLM-GLO

source_profiles.source_name: Society of Friends (Quaker) Meeting Records — Shelby County
source_profiles.repository: Earlham College Friends Collection, Richmond / Indiana Historical Society
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Monthly meeting records include births, marriages, deaths, removals (transfers), and disownments. Indiana Yearly Meeting and Western Yearly Meeting records. Quakers kept meticulous records. Indiana was a major destination for Quaker migration from the Carolinas and Virginia — check origin meeting records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Exceptionally well-preserved; many microfilmed by FamilySearch and held at Earlham College

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Rockport. Created 1818 from Perry and Warrick counties. Abraham Lincoln lived in Spencer County (1816-1830) — Lincoln Boyhood National Memorial and Lincoln State Park. Spencer County Historical Society. Lincoln family arrived via the Anderson River ferry. Ohio River county — steamboat and ferry records. Lincoln Pioneer Village. BLM-GLO federal land patents. Santa Claus, Indiana, post office handles Christmas mail annually. Dale and Grandview area records. Strong Baptist and Methodist church records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1818. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Indiana deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — earliest deeds may reference BLM-GLO original patents.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage applications, licenses, and returns from 1818. Indiana County Clerk issues marriage licenses. Applications include ages, birthplaces, and parents' names (improving detail over time).
source_profiles.survival_notes: Well-preserved at County Clerk office

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, administrations, guardianships, and estate inventories from 1818. Indiana probate is handled by the Circuit Court (County Clerk). Inventories often list personal property in detail and name heirs.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Probate records generally well-preserved at county level

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Civil and criminal case files, order books, and judgments from 1818. Indiana Circuit Court is the general trial court. Early records include debt cases, property disputes, bastardy cases, and naturalization petitions (pre-1906) that identify family relationships. Order books often record road petitions naming landowners.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Court records generally well-preserved at county level

source_profiles.repository: Spencer County Auditor, Rockport / Indiana State Archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Tax duplicates list property owners, land descriptions, and property values from 1818. Indiana county auditor records. Early tax lists serve as census substitutes. Indiana State Archives holds some microfilmed county tax records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Some tax duplicates at Indiana State Archives; others remain at county level

source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Original federal land patents for Spencer County. Indiana is a federal land state — most land was sold through federal land offices. Patents record first purchaser from the government. Searchable online at glorecords.blm.gov. Cross-reference with county deed records for subsequent transfers.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Complete federal records — well-preserved and digitized at BLM-GLO

source_profiles.source_name: German Catholic Parish Records — Spencer County
source_profiles.repository: Archdiocese of Indianapolis / Diocese of Evansville / Parish offices
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Baptism, marriage, and burial records from German Catholic parishes. Many parishes kept records in Latin or German. Parish registers often include parents' birthplaces in Germany. St. Meinrad Archabbey (Dubois/Spencer) holds significant church records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Parish records generally well-preserved; some microfilmed by FamilySearch

source_profiles.source_name: Ohio River Commerce Records — Spencer County
source_profiles.repository: Indiana State Archives / Local historical societies / Kentucky river communities
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Steamboat records, ferry licenses, river pilot records, and flatboat commerce. Ohio River counties had close economic ties to Kentucky — cross-reference Kentucky county records across the river. Early Indiana settlement came primarily via the Ohio River.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Ferry licenses at county level; steamboat records at NARA and historical societies

source_profiles.repository: Spencer County Clerk, Rockport / NARA Chicago
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Declarations of intention, petitions for naturalization, and certificates. Pre-1906 records at county Circuit Court (County Clerk). Post-1906 copies at NARA Chicago. Indiana had significant German, Irish, Swiss, and later Eastern European and Mexican immigration.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Many Indiana naturalization records indexed on FamilySearch and Ancestry; some at Indiana State Archives

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in South Bend. Created 1830 from Allen and unorganized Cass territory. South Bend is the home of the University of Notre Dame (1842) — university archives hold extensive local and Catholic records. Studebaker automobile and wagon company records document thousands of workers (1852-1963). St. Joseph County Historical Society. St. Joseph River valley settlements. Notre Dame's Hesburgh Libraries have significant genealogical materials. Oliver Corporation (farm equipment) records. Mishawaka and Walkerton area records. Strong Polish, Hungarian, and Belgian immigrant communities.

source_profiles.repository: St. Joseph County Recorder, South Bend
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1830. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Indiana deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — earliest deeds may reference BLM-GLO original patents.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.repository: St. Joseph County Clerk, South Bend
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage applications, licenses, and returns from 1830. Indiana County Clerk issues marriage licenses. Applications include ages, birthplaces, and parents' names (improving detail over time).
source_profiles.survival_notes: Well-preserved at County Clerk office

source_profiles.repository: St. Joseph County Clerk (Circuit/Superior Court), South Bend
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, administrations, guardianships, and estate inventories from 1830. Indiana probate is handled by the Circuit Court (County Clerk). Inventories often list personal property in detail and name heirs.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Probate records generally well-preserved at county level

source_profiles.source_name: Federal Land Patents (BLM-GLO) — St. Joseph County
source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Original federal land patents for St. Joseph County. Indiana is a federal land state — most land was sold through federal land offices. Patents record first purchaser from the government. Searchable online at glorecords.blm.gov. Cross-reference with county deed records for subsequent transfers.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Complete federal records — well-preserved and digitized at BLM-GLO

source_profiles.repository: NARA, Washington DC / Indiana State Archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Potawatomi Nation records including treaties, Trail of Death removal records (1838), land allotments, and agent correspondence. 859 Potawatomi were forcibly removed from Indiana to Kansas in September 1838. Chief Menominee's band. Pokagon Band of Potawatomi retained some lands.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Federal records at NARA Record Group 75; Trail of Death records well-documented

source_profiles.repository: St. Joseph County Clerk, South Bend / NARA Chicago
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Declarations of intention, petitions for naturalization, and certificates. Pre-1906 records at county Circuit Court (County Clerk). Post-1906 copies at NARA Chicago. Indiana had significant German, Irish, Swiss, and later Eastern European and Mexican immigration.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Many Indiana naturalization records indexed on FamilySearch and Ancestry; some at Indiana State Archives

source_profiles.source_name: Civil War Records — St. Joseph County
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Indiana provided approximately 197,000 soldiers to the Union Army. Adjutant General records, enlistment papers, and discharge records at Indiana State Archives. Indiana Soldiers and Sailors Monument (Indianapolis). W.H.H. Terrell's 'Report of the Adjutant General of the State of Indiana' (8 vols.) published rosters.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Indiana Adjutant General records well-preserved at Indiana State Archives; indexed online

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Knox, IN (not to be confused with Knox County). Created 1835 from St. Joseph County. Starke County Historical Society. Bass Lake and Koontz Lake area settlements. BLM-GLO federal land patents. North Judson and Hamlet area records. Kankakee River and Yellow River valleys. One of Indiana's less populous counties — limited but well-preserved records. Agricultural and lake-area county. Check St. Joseph County for pre-1835 records. Strong Catholic church records from German and Irish settlers.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1835. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Indiana deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — earliest deeds may reference BLM-GLO original patents.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Angola. Created 1835 from LaGrange County. Steuben County is in Indiana's lake region (over 100 lakes). Steuben County Historical Society. Lake James, Pokagon State Park area. Angola was a stop on the Underground Railroad. Tri-State University (now Trine University, 1884) archives. BLM-GLO federal land patents. Pokagon Band of Potawatomi connections — check tribal records. Fremont, Hamilton, and Orland area records. Check LaGrange County for pre-1835 records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1835. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Indiana deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — earliest deeds may reference BLM-GLO original patents.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.survival_notes: Well-preserved at County Clerk office

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, administrations, guardianships, and estate inventories from 1835. Indiana probate is handled by the Circuit Court (County Clerk). Inventories often list personal property in detail and name heirs.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Probate records generally well-preserved at county level

source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Original federal land patents for Steuben County. Indiana is a federal land state — most land was sold through federal land offices. Patents record first purchaser from the government. Searchable online at glorecords.blm.gov. Cross-reference with county deed records for subsequent transfers.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Complete federal records — well-preserved and digitized at BLM-GLO

source_profiles.source_name: Underground Railroad and African American Records — Steuben County
source_profiles.repository: Indiana State Archives / Indiana Historical Society / Levi Coffin House
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Indiana was a free state but had Black Codes restricting African American settlement. Court records include freedom suits, fugitive slave cases, and Black Code enforcement. Levi Coffin of Fountain City (Wayne County) was the 'President of the Underground Railroad.' Quaker meeting records document abolitionist activity.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Scattered across local historical societies and Indiana State Archives; some published accounts

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Sullivan. Created 1816 from Knox — one of the original counties at statehood. Sullivan County Historical Society. Major coal mining county — Sullivan County coal mine records document workers including Italian and Eastern European immigrants. Wabash River valley settlements. BLM-GLO federal land patents. Dugger, Shelburn, and Carlisle area records. Strong labor movement records — United Mine Workers active here. Shakamak State Park area. Check Knox County for pre-1816 records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1816. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Indiana deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — earliest deeds may reference BLM-GLO original patents.

source_profiles.source_name: Coal Mining Employment Records — Sullivan County
source_profiles.repository: Indiana State Archives / Indiana Coal Council / Local historical societies
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Indiana coal mine employment records, company town records, and United Mine Workers (UMW) union records. Coal mining attracted Italian, Eastern European, and African American workers. Mine inspection reports at Indiana State Archives list workers. Company store ledgers document families.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Mine records scattered; Indiana Bureau of Mines reports at Indiana State Archives

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Vevay. Created 1814 from Dearborn and Jefferson counties. Vevay was founded by Swiss immigrants (Jean Jacques Dufour's Swiss colony, 1802) — the oldest Swiss settlement in the US. Wine-growing heritage — early vineyard and viticulture records. Switzerland County Historical Society. Ohio River county. Edward Eggleston (author of 'The Hoosier Schoolmaster') heritage. BLM-GLO federal land patents. Patriot and East Enterprise area records. Swiss immigrant records at Indiana State Archives and Indiana Historical Society.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1814. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Indiana deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — earliest deeds may reference BLM-GLO original patents.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Lafayette. Created 1826 from Parke and Montgomery counties. Purdue University (1869) archives in West Lafayette hold extensive collections. Battle of Tippecanoe (1811) — battle records and Prophet's Town site at NARA and Indiana State Archives. Tippecanoe County Historical Association and Battlefield Museum. Wabash River valley. Fort Ouiatenon French colonial heritage. BLM-GLO federal land patents. Strong German and Irish immigrant records. Lafayette was a major railroad center — railroad employment records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1826. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Indiana deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — earliest deeds may reference BLM-GLO original patents.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wabash and Erie Canal (longest canal ever built in North America, 468 miles) and Whitewater Canal construction records, land purchases, worker rolls, and canal-related property records. Canal workers included many Irish immigrants. Canal land grants at Indiana State Archives.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Canal Commission records at Indiana State Archives; some published by Indiana Historical Bureau

source_profiles.survival_notes: Indiana Adjutant General records well-preserved at Indiana State Archives; indexed online

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Tipton. Created 1844 from Hamilton and Cass counties. Tipton County Historical Society. Cicero Creek valley settlements. BLM-GLO federal land patents. Small agricultural county with well-preserved records. Windfall was named for a tornado (wind-fall of trees) in 1830s. Kempton, Sharpsville, and Goldsmith area records. Strong Methodist and Christian Church records. Check Hamilton and Cass for pre-1844 records. Indiana's geographic center is near Tipton.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1844. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Indiana deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — earliest deeds may reference BLM-GLO original patents.
